question_text: 04 [14M]: An output restricted Deque front (ORDeque) is linear data structure, insert insert similar to queue, but in which insertion is delete allowed at both ends (pointed to by "front" and "rear") , whereas deletion is allowed only at one end, i.e. "rear" end: "front" points to the first free location where new element can be inserted, whereas "rear" points to the index of the element which should be deleted as a result of dequeue() operation: Consider the array implementation of a ORDeque with circular indexing; i.e if the queue is not full and rear points to the last index of array, Enqueue from "rear" end will insert the element at the first index of array: Assume array index starts from 1.

items.description: Checks if front = rear and reports that the queue is empty.
Extracts the element before updating the pointer by assigning x = Q[rear].
Updates rear circularly after dequeue: if rear = 1 then rear = Q.size else rear = rear - 1, then returns x.
Checks if the queue is full using isFull(Q), prints Queue Full, and returns before enqueueing.
For a = 0, inserts from rear by circularly updating rear: if rear = Q.size then rear = 1 else rear = rear + 1, then stores x in Q[rear].
For a = 1, inserts from front by setting Q[front] = x and circularly updating front: if front = 1 then front = Q.size else front = front - 1.
Checks the first ORDeque full condition and returns true when it holds.
Checks the second ORDeque full condition and otherwise returns false.
